nodes.title: Settle once and clean every terminal resource
nodes.why: Success, abort, timeout, and check failure can race; one idempotent path prevents duplicate work and leaks.
nodes.alternative: Repeat done-state and cleanup logic in every branch.
nodes.tradeoff: A small helper makes terminal behavior explicit and consistent.

nodes.title: Keep the deadline independent of check completion
nodes.why: A check Promise may never settle, so elapsed-time checks after await cannot implement a real deadline.
nodes.tradeoff: A second timer handle is required, but timeout no longer depends on check cooperation.

nodes.title: Guard immediately after await before retrying
nodes.why: Abort or timeout may win while check is pending; the resumed attempt must observe that before resolving or scheduling.
nodes.alternative: Let the in-flight attempt finish and enqueue one more timer.
nodes.tradeoff: Repeated guards are deliberate protection against async races.
